studyName: My Pediatric and Adult Rare Tumor (MyPART) Natural History Study of Rare Solid Tumors

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, and clinician-reported data from the Physical Exam Form collected during clinic visits, including age of patient at time of exam, performance score, height, weight, BMI, blood pressure, pulse, respiratory rate, body temperature, patient-reported pain, general exam impressions, results of examination of skin, ears, nose, throat, eyes, neck, heart, lung, breasts, abdomen, lymph nodes, pelvis, genitalia, extremities, strength, range of motion, and neurological function.

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, and clinician-reported data from the Review of Systems Form collected during clinic visits, including age of patient at time of exam, puberty, handedness, alcohol use, tobacco use, recreational drug use, sexual activity, constitutional symptoms, head, ears, eyes, nose, and throat symptoms, cardiovascular symptoms, respiratory symptoms, gastrointestinal symptoms, genitourinary symptoms, musculoskeletal symptoms, integumentary symptoms, neurological symptoms, endocrine symptoms, psychiatric symptoms, recent pain, and recent cancer-related events and treatments.

variables.description: Standardized T-score for PROMIS survey such that mean = 50 and std dev = 10

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
